---
title: 'Managing temporal and spatial data with Python'
jupyter: python3
---

## Setting up

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib as mpl
```

## Temporal data

```{python}
aord = pd.read_csv("data/AORD_5min.csv")
print(aord.head())
```

### Setting up time series data
Index vs column, how Python handles datetime, using `pd.Dataframe.dt`

```{python}

```
```{python}
# Convert to datetime
aord = pd.read_csv("data/AORD_5min.csv", parse_dates=["Datetime"])
aord.head()
```
dtypes
```{python}
aord.dtypes
```


### Filter 
October
```{python}
aord[aord["Datetime"].dt.month == 10]
```
### Resampling
Daily
```{python}
aord.resample("D", on="Datetime").sum()
```

Let's get rid of weekends
```{python}
aord[aord["Datetime"].dt.weekday < 5].resample("D", on="Datetime")
```

Alternatively, use the `"B"` resample. Note: this will pull weekend values into the Monday.

```{python}
weekdays = aord.resample("B", on="Datetime").agg(["max", "min"])
weekdays
```

### Index vs Column

```{python}
# Index vs column1111
weekdays.columns
```
```{python}
weekdays.index
```

```{python}
weekdays[weekdays.index.day == 3]
```


### Group by day
```{python}
weekdays.groupby(weekdays.index.day).agg(["mean", "std", "count"])
```


### Plotting

**Plot the prev groupby too...?**

```{python}
weekdays[[("High", "max"), ("Low", "min")]]
```

```{python}
weekdays[[("High", "max"), ("Low", "min")]].plot()
```

### Timezones
```{python}
aord["Datetime"].dt.tz_convert("Australia/Brisbane")
```

### Time formatting
See https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#strftime-and-strptime-behavior

```{python}
aord["Datetime"].dt.strftime("%c")
```


### Activity
Download the 1d data, and play


## Spatial data

```{python}
# import module
import geopandas as gpd
```

```{python}
# import the park polygons
parks = gpd.read_file("data/park-locations.geojson")

# see the contents
parks.head()
```

Some `gpd.GeoDataFrame` methods/attributes

```{python}

```

```{python}
# visualise (static)
parks.plot()
```

```{python}
parks.plot("park_size", legend=True)
```

```{python}
# what is the area of each polygon?
parks.area
```

```{python}
# re-project to do more precise calculations.
parks = parks.to_crs("EPSG:7856")
parks.area  # no more warning
# check CRS
```

```{python}
parks.crs

```

```{python}
# biggest ones?
parks.area.sort_values(ascending=False)
```

```{python}
# change the index to the park name
parks = parks.set_index("park_name")
parks.area.sort_values(ascending=False)
```

```{python}
# add the areas to the geodataframe
parks["area"] = parks.area
parks.plot("area", legend=True)
```

```{python}
parks.explore()
```

```{python}
# interactive plot
# (this needs folium and mapclassify)
interactive = parks.explore()
# export it to a file
interactive.save("interactive.html")
```

### Activity: List nearest parks to particular location


```{python}
# import toilets
toilets = gpd.read_file("data/public-toilets-in-brisbane.geojson")
# use same CRS as parks
toilets = toilets.to_crs(parks.crs)

toilets.plot()
```

```{python}
joined = gpd.sjoin(parks, toilets, how="left", predicate="contains")

```

```{python}
toilets_per_park = joined.groupby("park_name")["facilityid"].count()

parks = parks.merge(toilets_per_park, on="park_name", how="left")

parks = parks.rename(columns={"facilityid": "num_toilets"})
```

```{python}
parks.plot("num_toilets")

```

```{python}
all_toilets = toilets.dissolve()

parks.covers(all_toilets)
```

```{python}
parks["has_toilet"] = parks["num_toilets"] > 0

parks.plot("has_toilet", legend=True)
```

```{python}
parks.explore("has_toilet", legend=True)
```

Toilet density $\rho=\frac{N}{A}$.

```{python}
parks["toilet_density"] = parks["num_toilets"] / parks["area"]

parks.plot("toilet_density", norm=mpl.colors.LogNorm())
```

```{python}
parks.explore("toilet_density")
```
 
MAKE THIS LOG
